latin: Paulus exercitum Fremen duxit contra Harkonnenes et Imperatorem ipsum. tempestas harenae maxima urbem texit dum Fremen vermibus vecti muros oppugnaverunt. Paulus Feyd-Rautham Harkonnen in singulari certamine occidit. Imperator, victus et sine spe, thronum Paulo cedere coactus est. sic filius ducis interfecti rex totius imperii factus est. sed Paulus sciebat bellum sacrum quod timebat iam impediri non posse.

questions.prompt: Who did Paul lead the Fremen against?
questions.target: The Harkonnens and the Emperor himself
choices: The Harkonnens and the Emperor himself

questions.target: He killed him in single combat
choices: He killed him in single combat

questions.prompt: What happened to the Emperor after the battle?
questions.target: He was forced to give his throne to Paul
choices: He was forced to give his throne to Paul

questions.prompt: How did Paul feel about his victory?
questions.target: He knew the holy war he feared could no longer be stopped
choices: He knew the holy war he feared could no longer be stopped
He was relieved it was all over
He planned to return to Caladan

translation: Paul led the Fremen army against the Harkonnens and the Emperor himself. An enormous sandstorm covered the city while the Fremen, riding worms, attacked the walls. Paul killed Feyd-Rautha Harkonnen in single combat. The Emperor, defeated and without hope, was forced to yield his throne to Paul. Thus the son of the slain duke became ruler of the entire empire. But Paul knew that the holy war he feared could no longer be stopped.
